instruction: Generate PostgreSQL schema from this UI
Generate PostgreSQL schema from this UI
output:
CREATE TABLE users (
    user_id VARCHAR(255) PRIMARY KEY,
    user_name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    user_handle VARCHAR(255) UNIQUE NOT NULL,
    user_avatar_url VARCHAR(2048)
);

CREATE TABLE stories (
    story_id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    user_id VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL REFERENCES users(user_id),
    media_url VARCHAR(2048) NOT NULL,
    created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    expires_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE,
    is_highlight BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE
);

CREATE TABLE posts (
    post_id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    author_id VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL REFERENCES users(user_id),
    text_content TEXT,
    created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    link_preview_url VARCHAR(2048),
    link_preview_title VARCHAR(255),
    link_preview_description TEXT,
    link_preview_image_url VARCHAR(2048)
);

CREATE TABLE media_attachments (
    media_attachment_id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    post_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES posts(post_id),
    media_url VARCHAR(2048) NOT NULL,
    media_format VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL, -- 'image' or 'video'
    created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

CREATE TABLE polls (
    poll_id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    post_id UUID UNIQUE NOT NULL REFERENCES posts(post_id),
    question_text TEXT NOT NULL,
    created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

CREATE TABLE poll_options (
    poll_option_id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    poll_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES polls(poll_id),
    option_text VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    option_order INTEGER NOT NULL,
    UNIQUE (poll_id, option_order)
);

CREATE TABLE poll_votes (
    poll_vote_id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    poll_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES polls(poll_id),
    poll_option_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES poll_options(poll_option_id),
    user_id VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL REFERENCES users(user_id),
    voted_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    UNIQUE (poll_id, user_id)
);

CREATE TABLE post_locations (
    post_location_id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    post_id UUID UNIQUE NOT NULL REFERENCES posts(post_id),
    location_name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    latitude DECIMAL(9,6),
    longitude DECIMAL(9,6),
    created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

CREATE TABLE post_likes (
    post_like_id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    post_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES posts(post_id),
    user_id VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL REFERENCES users(user_id),
    liked_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    UNIQUE (post_id, user_id)
);

CREATE TABLE post_shares (
    post_share_id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    post_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES posts(post_id),
    user_id VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL REFERENCES users(user_id),
    shared_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    UNIQUE (post_id, user_id)
);

CREATE TABLE post_views (
    post_view_id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    post_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES posts(post_id),
    user_id VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL REFERENCES users(user_id),
    viewed_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

CREATE TABLE post_bookmarks (
    post_bookmark_id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    post_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES posts(post_id),
    user_id VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL REFERENCES users(user_id),
    bookmarked_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    UNIQUE (post_id, user_id)
);

CREATE TABLE comments (
    comment_id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    post_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES posts(post_id),
    author_id VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL REFERENCES users(user_id),
    comment_text TEXT NOT NULL,
    created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    parent_comment_id UUID REFERENCES comments(comment_id)
);

CREATE TABLE comment_likes (
    comment_like_id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    comment_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES comments(comment_id),
    user_id VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL REFERENCES users(user_id),
    liked_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    UNIQUE (comment_id, user_id)
);

CREATE TABLE users (
    id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
    email VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    password_hash VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    first_name VARCHAR(100),
    last_name VARCHAR(100),
    created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    updated_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

CREATE TABLE products (
    id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
    name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    description TEXT,
    unit_price NUMERIC(10, 2) NOT NULL,
    created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    updated_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

CREATE TABLE carts (
    id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
    user_id INTEGER REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
    subtotal NUMERIC(10, 2) NOT NULL DEFAULT 0.00,
    shipping_cost NUMERIC(10, 2) NOT NULL DEFAULT 0.00,
    tax_amount NUMERIC(10, 2) NOT NULL DEFAULT 0.00,
    discount_amount NUMERIC(10, 2) NOT NULL DEFAULT 0.00,
    total_amount NUMERIC(10, 2) NOT NULL DEFAULT 0.00,
    current_checkout_step VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'shipping_step',
    status VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'active',
    created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    updated_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

CREATE TABLE cart_items (
    id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
    cart_id INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES carts(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    product_id INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES products(id),
    quantity INTEGER NOT NULL CHECK (quantity > 0),
    price_at_purchase NUMERIC(10, 2) NOT NULL,
    line_item_total NUMERIC(10, 2) NOT NULL,
    created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    updated_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

instruction: Generate PostgreSQL schema from this UI

CREATE TYPE invoice_status_enum AS ENUM (
    'Draft',
    'Pending Approval',
    'Sent',
    'Partially Paid',
    'Paid',
    'Overdue',
    'Cancelled'
);

CREATE TYPE invoice_currency_enum AS ENUM (
    'USD',
    'EUR',
    'GBP',
    'CAD',
    'AUD'
);

CREATE TABLE customers (
    customer_id VARCHAR(50) PRIMARY KEY
);

CREATE TABLE invoices (
    invoice_id VARCHAR(50) PRIMARY KEY,
    status invoice_status_enum NOT NULL DEFAULT 'Draft',
    issue_date DATE NOT NULL,
    due_date DATE NOT NULL,
    currency invoice_currency_enum NOT NULL DEFAULT 'USD',
    customer_id VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
    FOREIGN KEY (customer_id) REFERENCES customers(customer_id)
);

instruction: Analyze the UI structure and generate an appropriate database schema.
output:
CREATE TABLE Users (
  id INT PRIMARY KEY AUTO_INCREMENT,
  name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
  headline VARCHAR(500),
  about_me TEXT,
  profile_picture_url VARCHAR(255),
  email VARCHAR(255) UNIQUE NOT NULL,
  phone_number VARCHAR(20),
  website_url VARCHAR(255),
  connections_count INT DEFAULT 0,
  created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
  updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

CREATE TABLE Experiences (
  id INT PRIMARY KEY AUTO_INCREMENT,
  user_id INT NOT NULL,
  title VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
  company VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
  start_date DATE NOT NULL,
  end_date DATE,
  description TEXT,
  FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES Users(id)
);

CREATE TABLE Educations (
  id INT PRIMARY KEY AUTO_INCREMENT,
  user_id INT NOT NULL,
  degree VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
  institution VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
  field_of_study VARCHAR(255),
  start_date DATE NOT NULL,
  end_date DATE,
  FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES Users(id)
);

CREATE TABLE Skills (
  id INT PRIMARY KEY AUTO_INCREMENT,
  name VARCHAR(100) UNIQUE NOT NULL
);

CREATE TABLE UserSkills (
  user_id INT NOT NULL,
  skill_id INT NOT NULL,
  PRIMARY KEY (user_id, skill_id),
  FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES Users(id),
  FOREIGN KEY (skill_id) REFERENCES Skills(id)
);

instruction: Analyze the UI structure and generate an appropriate database schema.
output:
CREATE TABLE Developers (
  id INT PRIMARY KEY AUTO_INCREMENT,
  name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
  bio TEXT,
  profile_picture_url VARCHAR(255),
  github_url VARCHAR(255),
  linkedin_url VARCHAR(255),
  personal_website_url VARCHAR(255),
  created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
  updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

CREATE TABLE Projects (
  id INT PRIMARY KEY AUTO_INCREMENT,
  developer_id INT NOT NULL,
  title VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
  description TEXT,
  tech_stack VARCHAR(500), -- Comma-separated or JSON array for simplicity
  live_demo_url VARCHAR(255),
  github_repo_url VARCHAR(255),
  created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
  FOREIGN KEY (developer_id) REFERENCES Developers(id)
);

CREATE TABLE DeveloperSkills (
  id INT PRIMARY KEY AUTO_INCREMENT,
  developer_id INT NOT NULL,
  skill_name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
  FOREIGN KEY (developer_id) REFERENCES Developers(id)
);

CREATE TABLE Activities (
  id INT PRIMARY KEY AUTO_INCREMENT,
  developer_id INT NOT NULL,
  activity_type VARCHAR(50), -- e.g., 'commit', 'blog_post'

  activity_url VARCHAR(255),
  activity_date TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
  FOREIGN KEY (developer_id) REFERENCES Developers(id)
);

instruction: Analyze the UI structure and generate an appropriate database schema.
output:
CREATE TABLE Customers (
  id INT PRIMARY KEY AUTO_INCREMENT,
  first_name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
  last_name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
  email VARCHAR(255) UNIQUE NOT NULL,
  phone_number VARCHAR(20),
  created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
  updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

CREATE TABLE Addresses (
  id INT PRIMARY KEY AUTO_INCREMENT,
  customer_id INT NOT NULL,
  address_line1 VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
  address_line2 VARCHAR(255),
  city VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
  state VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
  zip_code VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL,
  country VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
  is_default BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
  FOREIGN KEY (customer_id) REFERENCES Customers(id)
);

CREATE TABLE PaymentMethods (
  id INT PRIMARY KEY AUTO_INCREMENT,
  customer_id INT NOT NULL,
  card_type VARCHAR(50),
  last_four_digits VARCHAR(4),
  expiration_date DATE,
  is_default BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
  -- tokenized_payment_data TEXT NOT NULL, -- For secure storage
  FOREIGN KEY (customer_id) REFERENCES Customers(id)
);

CREATE TABLE Orders (
  id INT PRIMARY KEY AUTO_INCREMENT,
  customer_id INT NOT NULL,
  order_date TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
  total_amount DECIMAL(10, 2) NOT NULL,
  status VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
  shipping_address_id INT,
  FOREIGN KEY (customer_id) REFERENCES Customers(id),
  FOREIGN KEY (shipping_address_id) REFERENCES Addresses(id)
);

CREATE TABLE Products (
  id INT PRIMARY KEY AUTO_INCREMENT,
  name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
  description TEXT,
  price DECIMAL(10, 2) NOT NULL,
  image_url VARCHAR(255)
);

CREATE TABLE WishlistItems (
  id INT PRIMARY KEY AUTO_INCREMENT,
  customer_id INT NOT NULL,
  product_id INT NOT NULL,
  added_date TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
  FOREIGN KEY (customer_id) REFERENCES Customers(id),
  FOREIGN KEY (product_id) REFERENCES Products(id)
);

CREATE TABLE Reviews (
  id INT PRIMARY KEY AUTO_INCREMENT,
  customer_id INT NOT NULL,
  product_id INT NOT NULL,
  rating INT CHECK (rating >= 1 AND rating <= 5),
  comment TEXT,
  review_date TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
  FOREIGN KEY (customer_id) REFERENCES Customers(id),
  FOREIGN KEY (product_id) REFERENCES Products(id)
);

instruction: Analyze the UI structure and generate an appropriate database schema.
output:
CREATE TABLE Players (
  id INT PRIMARY KEY AUTO_INCREMENT,
  gamertag VARCHAR(255) UNIQUE NOT NULL,
  avatar_url VARCHAR(255),
  bio TEXT,
  current_level INT DEFAULT 1,
  total_playtime_minutes INT DEFAULT 0,
  created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
  updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

CREATE TABLE Games (
  id INT PRIMARY KEY AUTO_INCREMENT,
  title VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
  genre VARCHAR(100),
  cover_art_url VARCHAR(255),
  release_date DATE
);

CREATE TABLE PlayerGames (
  id INT PRIMARY KEY AUTO_INCREMENT,
  player_id INT NOT NULL,
  game_id INT NOT NULL,
  playtime_minutes INT DEFAULT 0,
  purchase_date DATE,
  FOREIGN KEY (player_id) REFERENCES Players(id),
  FOREIGN KEY (game_id) REFERENCES Games(id)
);

CREATE TABLE Achievements (
  id INT PRIMARY KEY AUTO_INCREMENT,
  game_id INT NOT NULL,
  name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
  description TEXT,
  icon_url VARCHAR(255),
  FOREIGN KEY (game_id) REFERENCES Games(id)
);

CREATE TABLE PlayerAchievements (
  player_id INT NOT NULL,
  achievement_id INT NOT NULL,
  unlocked_date TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
  PRIMARY KEY (player_id, achievement_id),
  FOREIGN KEY (player_id) REFERENCES Players(id),
  FOREIGN KEY (achievement_id) REFERENCES Achievements(id)
);

CREATE TABLE Friends (
  player_id1 INT NOT NULL,
  player_id2 INT NOT NULL,
  status VARCHAR(50), -- e.g., 'pending', 'accepted'
  PRIMARY KEY (player_id1, player_id2),
  FOREIGN KEY (player_id1) REFERENCES Players(id),
  FOREIGN KEY (player_id2) REFERENCES Players(id)
);

CREATE TABLE GameStatistics (
  id INT PRIMARY KEY AUTO_INCREMENT,
  player_id INT NOT NULL,
  game_id INT NOT NULL,
  stat_name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
  stat_value DECIMAL(10, 2) NOT NULL,
  last_updated TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
  FOREIGN KEY (player_id) REFERENCES Players(id),
  FOREIGN KEY (game_id) REFERENCES Games(id)
);

instruction: Analyze the UI structure and generate an appropriate database schema.
output:
CREATE TABLE FitnessUsers (
  id INT PRIMARY KEY AUTO_INCREMENT,
  name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
  email VARCHAR(255) UNIQUE NOT NULL,
  profile_picture_url VARCHAR(255),
  gender VARCHAR(10),
  birth_date DATE,
  created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
  updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

CREATE TABLE UserMetrics (
  id INT PRIMARY KEY AUTO_INCREMENT,
  user_id INT NOT NULL,
  measurement_date DATE NOT NULL,
  weight_kg DECIMAL(5, 2),
  height_cm DECIMAL(5, 2),
  bmi DECIMAL(4, 2),
  body_fat_percentage DECIMAL(4, 2),
  FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES FitnessUsers(id)
);

CREATE TABLE Goals (
  id INT PRIMARY KEY AUTO_INCREMENT,
  user_id INT NOT NULL,
  goal_type VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL, -- e.g., 'weight_loss', 'steps_per_day'
  target_value DECIMAL(10, 2) NOT NULL,
  start_date DATE NOT NULL,

    description TEXT,
    image_url VARCHAR(255),
    product_count INT DEFAULT 0, -- Denormalized count or calculated
    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    FOREIGN KEY (parent_category_id) REFERENCES Categories(category_id)
);

instruction: Analyze the UI structure and generate an appropriate database schema.
output:
CREATE TABLE Reviews (
    review_id INT PRIMARY KEY AUTO_INCREMENT,
    product_id INT NOT NULL,
    user_id INT NOT NULL, -- Assumes a 'Users' table exists
    rating INT CHECK (rating >= 1 AND rating <= 5) NOT NULL,
    review_title VARCHAR(255),
    review_text TEXT,
    is_verified_purchase BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
    helpful_votes INT DEFAULT 0,
    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    FOREIGN KEY (product_id) REFERENCES Products(product_id),
    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES Users(user_id)
);

instruction: Analyze the UI structure and generate an appropriate database schema.
output:
CREATE TABLE InventoryMetrics (
    item_id INT PRIMARY KEY AUTO_INCREMENT,
    name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    sku VARCHAR(100) UNIQUE NOT NULL,
    current_stock INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    min_stock_level INT DEFAULT 0,
    max_stock_level INT DEFAULT 0,
    supplier_id INT, -- Foreign key to Suppliers
    status VARCHAR(50) GENERATED ALWAYS AS (
        CASE
            WHEN current_stock <= min_stock_level AND current_stock > 0 THEN 'Low Stock'
            WHEN current_stock = 0 THEN 'Out of Stock'
            ELSE 'In Stock'
        END
    ) STORED, -- Computed column for stock status (syntax may vary by DB, e.g., PostgreSQL uses 'GENERATED ALWAYS AS ... STORED')
    last_reorder_date DATE,
    last_updated TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    FOREIGN KEY (supplier_id) REFERENCES Suppliers(supplier_id)
);

-- Auxiliary table for suppliers (implied for full functionality):
-- CREATE TABLE Suppliers (
--     supplier_id INT PRIMARY KEY AUTO_INCREMENT,
--     supplier_name VARCHAR(255) UNIQUE NOT NULL
-- );

);
